title: triage_verdict
description: Standardized verdict produced by the triaging room (triage-only mode). Answers ONE question: does the reported bug still exist in the current working tree, with concrete code evidence — without running the fix pipeline.

verdict.description: ALREADY-FIXED: the cited defect no longer exists (the fix / a regression test is in the tree). STILL-LIVE: the buggy code/behavior is still present. PARTIAL: some sites fixed, others remain. UNCLEAR: could not determine from the code (needs a human or a live repro).

confidence.description: 0..1 confidence in the verdict, given the code evidence examined.

summary_title.description: One-line verdict headline, e.g. 'ALREADY-FIXED — severity sort landed in localfiles_ticket.go'.

evidence.description: Concrete code evidence: the file:line / function / regression test examined and what was found there (the buggy line still reads X, or function Y now does Z, or TestW asserts the fix). This is the load-bearing field — it must cite specific code, not prose.

summary_markdown.description: Human-readable markdown explaining the verdict and the evidence trail, delivered to the transport / inbox.

suggested_action.description: What to do next: e.g. 'close as fixed (commit <sha>)', 'drive the full bugfix pipeline', 'needs human repro', 'fix the remaining site in <file>'.

fixed_in_ref.description: When ALREADY-FIXED: the commit SHA / function / test that proves the fix, if identifiable. Null otherwise.
